feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: DELIVERYDATEQUANTITYISFIXED not available in Incoming Sales Orders - Flexible Analysis
entry.content: For analytics reasons (delivery goals) we need the field DELIVERYDATEQUANTITYISFIXED to be selectable as "Available Fields" in App "Incoming Sales Orders - Flexible Analysis". DELIVERYDATEQUANTITYISFIXED (delivery date and quantity is fixed) is part of CDS Views "I_SALESDOCUMENTITEM", from which a lot of other fields are availible to select in the App already - but this is missing. Is there a away to add Fields from this CDS View?

entry.title: VAT ID x country mismatch: impact on tax determination, billing output and reporting
entry.content:
Hi experts,

we are currently facing a question regarding VAT handling in Public Cloud and would appreciate your input.

In our current setup, the system checks whether the VAT ID of a customer matches the country of the business partner.
If there is a mismatch (e.g. customer based in Ireland but VAT ID from Austria), the system treats the VAT ID as invalid.

As a consequence:

  • a different tax determination is applied 
  • the customer is processed as if no valid VAT ID exists
  • the VAT ID is not printed on the billing document, even though it is maintained in the master data

From a system logic perspective, this behavior is understandable.

However, according to our customer, the business expectation is different:
The customer should still be treated as having a valid VAT ID (as one is actually maintained), regardless of the country mismatch.

This would imply:

  • a different tax determination 
  • and that the VAT ID should be printed on the billing document

Our key questions:

  • Is there a recommended way to handle such scenarios in standard?
  • How does this situation impact VAT reporting (e.g. EC Sales List or similar declarations)?
  • Does anyone know how this affects tax reporting / payment declarations in practice?

We want to ensure that both document output and legal reporting are consistent and compliant.

Any insights or experiences would be highly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!
Katharina

entry.title: Situation Handling for Journal Entry Posting – Triggering Issue with CL_FINS_JOURNALENTRY_SOT_EVENT
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Finance Situation Handling 

Dear experts,

I am working with Situation Handling in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and have a requirement to notify specific users via Fiori notification or email when a Journal Entry is posted, specifically based on criteria such as specific G/L accounts or Journal Entry Types.

I have completed the configuration for the following components:

  • Responsibility Management: Team and functions defined.

  • Situation Object / Scenario / Type: Configured and enabled the Situation Type.

However, despite these settings, the situation is not triggering as expected. When checking the Monitor Situations app and Application Logs, there is no evidence of any instance being created or any error logs being generated.

In my technical investigation, I am focusing on the anchor object class CL_FINS_JOURNALENTRY_SOT_EVENT. However, it is unclear exactly how or when this class is triggered within the Public Cloud environment.

My questions are:

  1. Triggering Mechanism: Does the ABAP event class CL_FINS_JOURNALENTRY_SOT_EVENT trigger the situation in real-time immediately upon Journal Entry posting (Save event)? Or does it require a background job scheduled via the "Schedule Situation Handling Jobs" app?

  2. Standard Workaround or Recommended Approach: Is there a specific standard Situation Scenario or Job Template recommended for triggering notifications based on Journal Entry attributes in Public Cloud?

  3. Known Limitation by Design: Is the direct triggering of situations from the Journal Entry posting event a known limitation in the current version of SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud?

The goal is to inform relevant stakeholders immediately when a specific posting occurs using the standard Situation Handling framework, avoiding custom BAdI developments where possible.

Any guidance, best practices, or confirmation regarding the behavior of this specific class and situation object would be greatly appreciated.

Regards,
M. Kitakatsu

entry.title: In SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud Can We Create ODN Number Range Based On Sold To Party
entry.content:
Hello,

In SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition can we create ODN number range based on Sold To Party (Customer)

Generally we are using business place but customer want ODN number range based on Sold to Party for few customer required specific ODN number range should hit.

Kindly provide the suggestion on the addressed issue.

Regards,

Ashish

entry.title: Supplier rebate / allowance scenario in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hi all,

I am trying to understand the best-practice way to handle a supplier rebate / allowance scenario in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Our case is not only supplier-based, but also product-structure based. For example, a product may belong to a hierarchy like: Machines → Electronics → Hovers → Phonex. An agreement may apply at different levels, such as a specific product, a subgroup like Hovers, or a higher group like Electronics.

Also, we may buy the same product from different suppliers, and each supplier may have different conditions for that same product or hierarchy level. For example:

  • Supplier A gives 10% rebate for Hovers if purchase quantity or value reaches a certain level
  • Supplier B has a different rule for the same Hovers
  • A supplier may also give a fixed monthly amount for display/shelf space
  • Some suppliers or products may have no special agreement

From what I understand, the standard area in SAP for this is Settlement Management using supplier condition contracts, including settlement of supplier rebates and fixed-amount agreements.

My questions are:

  1. Is this the correct standard approach in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?
  2. Can the agreement logic be maintained by supplier + product / product group / hierarchy level?
  3. For fixed monthly amounts like display support, is it usually handled as part of the same supplier settlement logic, or as a separate process?
  4. If part of the agreement depends on our sales to customers instead of only purchasing-related data, is that supported in standard Public Cloud?

My goal is to make sure we can clearly track:

  • what we pay each supplier
  • what we should receive back as rebate/credit
  • and how this is settled in SAP

I would appreciate guidance from anyone who implemented a similar scenario in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

entry.title: Is possible to change the default document type for Advanced foreign currency valuation document
entry.content:
Dear Expert,

 

We need to change the document type of the 'Advanced foreign currency valuation' journal entries, as the default document type is 'SA'. However, 'SA' is also used in general journal entries, so we need to separate the 'Advanced foreign currency valuation' entries by document type.

 

Is this possible, and if so, how? Thank you.

entry.title: overview functionality public vs private cloud
entry.content:
Hello,

We are in the process of choosing public vs private cloud for a new implementation. Where can i find an overview of what functionality is available for finance and controlling on both platforms?  From what i was able to gather for example, costing sheet is available for both, but overhead template (CPTA/CPTD/CPT1...) only for private cloud. similat for costing based copa, that is not available on public cloud.

entry.title: Deactivation of Company Code
entry.content:
Hello Team,

 

we would like to deactivate a company code in Q and P-system.

we are not sure when we deactivate the company code. the data in the system will be available until how many years and can data be accessible for users if they have access due to audit purpose.

Can provide any links for this process in the SAP.

Do we have achieving approach in sap public cloud and whether this archiving happens in same system...?

entry.title: MASS UPLOAD AND POST GR/IR IN SAP S/4HANA PUBLIC CLOUD
entry.content: I have brought in material inventory balance for initial stock as at December 2025, I want to do mass GR/IR for the products to properly capture the goods movement that have happened from January 2026 to February 2026 so as to have an accurate balance

entry.title: CLM - Contract and Lease Management Migration in Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi,

Our customer was previously using RE-FX in S/4 for IFRS16 valuation and is moving to Public Cloud CLM.

We are in migration and cut-over phase and have migrated many contracts into CLM using the migration cockpit and simulated valuation successfully, but one contract generates a negative interest calculation error.

All the contracts were migrated with two statistical one-time conditions:

  • Asset Default Value - net worth value specified
  • Net present value (NPV) default - present value specified

For this lease contract the NPV value is > Contract value (value of the future lease payments considered for IFRS16).

Net Present Value Specified = 96,898.99

Contract Value = 76,762.75

Asset Default Value = 70,533.74 (ROU asset NBV)

 

When entering the valuation parameters, the interest rate entered is ignored due to the NPV condition value being specified.  As per SAP note, 3041660 the system calculates this interest rate automatically, based on NPV.

3041660 - 'Interest Rate' under 'Valuation Parameters' tab is different from valuation - SAP for Me

 

The error encountered during valuation is exactly as stipulated in this sap note, 3531206:

3531206 - RE-FX: RECEPR000, RECEPR116, RECEPR151, RECEPR502, RECEPR503, RECEPR506 when the Present Value Specified is used - SAP for Me

As per SAP note this error occurs where the present value exceeds the contract value, leading to an incorrect interest calculation based on a negative amount.  The Present Value Specified amount must be equal to or less than the contract value. 

 

Question – would you have any suggestions as to how to approach or resolve this migration issue?

 

Thanks

Sonya

 Other notes I looked at:

3626019 - Valuation Parameters negative interest - SAP for Me

3653143 - Central KBA: Interest Calculation in RE-FX - SAP for Me

entry.title: Guidance Required On Mapping Bankgiro and Plusgiro Payment Methods for Swedish Operations
entry.content:
As part of our Accounts Payable process for our Swedish operations, we are required to maintain Bankgiro and Plusgiro account numbers for our suppliers. However, we need clarification on the following:

  1. Where exactly should the Bankgiro and Plusgiro numbers be maintained in the Supplier Master Data — specifically, which fields under the Payment Transactions tab (Company Code level or General Data level) are designated for this purpose?

  2. How should the mapping be configured — is there a specific format, field assignment, or partner bank type that needs to be used to differentiate between Bankgiro and Plusgiro accounts?

  3. Are there any prerequisite configurations (e.g., bank key setup, payment method configuration, or house bank settings) that need to be in place before mapping these account numbers?

  4. Does this mapping require any specific country-specific settings for Sweden (Country Key: SE) to be activated?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Overhead is Zero on Event-Based Costing
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m currently checking the Order Cost Details – Event-Based app. There are no errors, but I don’t see any overhead costs being posted.

The costing sheet appears to be set up correctly, as the overhead is included in the Create Material Cost Estimate app.

Has anyone encountered a similar issue or could suggest which setting I might be missing?

Thanks a lot in advance!

entry.title: How to Implement Digital Signatures in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud (Invoices & POs)?
entry.content:
Hi SAP Experts,

We have a requirement to implement digital signatures for business documents such as Tax Invoices and Purchase Orders (print outputs) in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

While exploring possible solutions, I came across SAP Signature Management by DocuSign and reviewed the following resources:

  • https://me.sap.com/processnavigator/SolS/EARL_SolS-013/2602/SolP/1XV?region=IN

  • https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/ff01156df84f470cb46483b8a0ba0b79/2d27519421144e92bc09cb223ab18c1e.html?locale=en-US

I have a few questions:

  1. Is SAP Signature Management by DocuSign supported for SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud output documents (like invoices and POs)?

  2. What is the end-to-end process/architecture to implement this (especially for print forms)?

  3. Does it require SAP BTP integration or middleware for this scenario?

  4. Are there any standard SAP-supported approaches for embedding digital signatures directly in output forms (Adobe Forms)?

  5. If DocuSign is not feasible, what are the alternative approaches recommended in Public Cloud?

Any guidance, implementation steps, or real project experience would be highly appreciated.

Thanks in advance for your support!

Thanks,

Sai Bhargav

entry.title: AI Unit Cost per Request for Sales Order Creation (S/4HANA Public Cloud)
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I would like to confirm my understanding of AI Unit–based pricing for the following feature:

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition – Sales order creation from unstructured data

From the Discovery Center, I see:

  • AI Units per request: 0.1
  • Price per AI Unit: EUR 7

Based on this, my understanding is:

  • Cost per request = 0.1 × 7 = EUR 0.7
  • Therefore, if I create 100 sales orders, the total cost would be:
    → 100 × 0.7 = EUR 70

Could you please confirm if this calculation is correct?

Also, I would like to clarify:

  • Does one “request” always correspond to one sales order creation?
  • Are there any additional AI Unit consumptions (e.g., document parsing, retries, or workflow steps) that should be considered?

Additionally, I checked the AI Unit price list for my TDD environment, but I could not find this specific feature listed there.
Could you please advise where I can confirm the exact AI Unit pricing for this feature in TDD?

Thanks in advance for your support!

entry.title: SAP Public Cloud 2602 /Authorization Concept for the App List of Supplier Invoices
entry.content:
Hello Experts,
i need help with the following scenario:
The goal is, that a user can access the app List of supplier invoices and that he can view all invoices and attachements, but cant create, edit or cancel anything. The second aspect is, that the user should still be able to recieve workflow tasks for invoices that are Saved as Complete and is able to accept or deny them. 

The issue for in this case is, if i change the custom business role, that i created, and restrict the role to only read access and value help access, the user is no longer able to recieve and work with workflow tasks (as this counts as an edit to the supplier invoice).

My second idea was to create an app variant and remove unwanted buttons. This almost worked but the user is still able to cancel posted invoices, if he uses the app list of supplier invoices. If the user navigates to the invoice from the workflow task, then he enters F7900 and can even post or edit supplier invoices. Based on my tests, im not even able to make UI Adaptions to F7900 and prohibit the user from using these functions.

My question is, how can i proceed from here. Are there any other tools/settings that allow me to restrict the user on this granular level ? Can some restrictiontypes be used for this and not only for different access levels for example by Purchase Organization ? 

Im thankfull for any suggestions.

Best regards
Lukas

entry.title: "Tax account could not be determined for YCOA/&/&/&/&" - (FOT_TXA020)
entry.content:
When releasing a billing document to accounting in App Change Billing Document (VF02) the system raises error “Tax Account could not be determined for YCOA/&/&/&/&” Message no. FOT_TXA020

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Best Practice for Sales Service with Inbound Material Flow in S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm looking for the "Fit-to-Standard" approach in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for a specific business case:

  1. Service Sales: We provide a waste collection service.

  2. Reverse Logistics: Instead of shipping goods, we receive material (waste) from the customer into our plant.

  3. Invoicing: We need to invoice the customer for the collection service (Debit Memo).

In the legacy ECC, they used a "Sales Order -> Inbound Delivery" workaround. Since we want to keep the Clean Core, what is the recommended Scope Item or flow?

  • Should I use Advanced Customer Returns (23B) and trigger a Debit Memo instead of a Credit Memo?

  • Is there a way to combine Lean Services (J14) with an automated inbound movement?

  • Or is there a specific "Reverse Logistics for Service" scenario in Public Cloud?

Thanks in advance for your help!

entry.title: Billing Plan Restrictions in Quantity Contracts
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

I am currently working on implementing a rental contract scenario in SAP Public Cloud.

In SAP On-Premise, we used rental contracts (e.g. periodic billing scenarios via contracts with billing plans). However, in the Public Cloud, the available contract types are mainly limited to Quantity Contracts and Value Contracts.

1. Mapping of Rental Contract Scenario

Given this limitation, I would like to better understand the recommended approach:

  • What is the recommended standard object in SAP Public Cloud to represent a rental / periodic billing contract?
  • Should this scenario be modeled using Quantity Contracts with Billing Plans, or is there a more suitable alternative (e.g. subscription-based solutions (subscription billing) or other scope items)?

2. Billing Plan on Header vs. Item Level

In the Public Cloud system, I noticed that the Billing Plan can only be maintained at item level.
In SAP On-Premise, it was possible to maintain Billing Plans at header level as well.

  • Is there any configuration or workaround in the Public Cloud to enable Billing Plans at header level for Quantity Contracts?

3. Item Category Change Requirement

When creating a Billing Plan, it appears necessary to change the item category in order to enable billing plan functionality.

  • Is this the standard and expected behavior in SAP Public Cloud?
  • Are there recommended best practices to avoid manual item category adjustments?

4. Billing Plan Type Restrictions

The system defaults the Billing Plan Type to S5, and I am unable to modify it.

  • In SAP On-Premise, it was possible to define and assign different Billing Plan Types.
  • Is the Billing Plan Type fixed in the Public Cloud?
  • If so, is there any SSCUI or configuration path that allows influencing or extending the available Billing Plan Types?

Thank you in advance for your support.

Best regards,
Kathi

entry.title: Approval Workflow Supplier Invoice add Custom fields
entry.content:
Hello there lovely Community, 

We are using Approval workflows for Supplier invoices. Id like to adjsut the view for the myinbox app. More precise : id like to add a custom field to the view. 

I did that once for Purchase Orders: Here i did reaserch a deeplink to a Purchase order display deeplink app and added a customer field there -> also showed in my inbox. 

But for supplier invocie im Struggling at the moment.  

Anyone got a good idea?

 

 

kind regards, 

Philipp

entry.title: 5HP+MTO how to realize the 5HP+MTO function
entry.content:
5HP+MTO how to realize the 5HP+MTO function
 

We activated 5HP 5W2, and the production mode is MTO, but after creating a NIBC advanced inter-company purchase order, the production run MRP cannot generate the demand for E inventory. We do not know how to proceed with the testing. Could you please help guide us on how to implement the 5HP MTO mode in detail? Thank you very much.
